papers.title: Nearby Low-Mass Hypervelocity Stars
papers.author_names: Yanqiong Zhang Martin C. Smith Jeffrey L. Carlin

abstract.text: Hypervelocity stars are those that have speeds exceeding the escape speed and are hence unbound from the Milky Way. We investigate a sample of low-mass hypervelocity candidates obtained using data from the high-precision SDSS Stripe 82 catalogue, which we have combined with spectroscopy from the 200-inch Hale Telescope at Palomar Observatory. We find four good candidates, but without metallicities it is difficult to pin-down their distances and therefore total velocities. Our best candidate has a significant likelihood that it is escaping the Milky Way for a wide-range of metallicities.

brief.tldr: [astronomical research paper] The authors identify four low-mass hypervelocity star (HVS) candidates using high-precision proper motion data from the SDSS Stripe 82 survey, combined with spectroscopic radial velocity measurements from the Hale Telescope. Due to lack of metallicity data for K/M-type stars, distances and total velocities are estimated under a conservative [Fe/H] = -2 dex assumption, yielding total velocities exceeding 400 km/s for the best candidates; one candidate (NHCa) shows a high likelihood of being unbound from the Milky Way across a wide range of metallicities, suggesting a possible origin via tidal disruption by the galactic center.

source_fields.sections_text: Introduction: This paper introduces a novel search for low-mass hypervelocity star candidates using Stripe 82 data, addressing the gap in identifying such stars beyond O/B-type stars that are inconsistent with Galactic centre ejection scenarios. Data and methods: The paper proposes a method to identify nearby hypervelocity star candidates by selecting red main-sequence stars from the Stripe 82 dataset, excluding white dwarfs, assuming a low metallicity to estimate distances, and filtering for high tangential velocities and low proper motion errors, followed by spectroscopic confirmation of 3D velocities. Candidate hypervelocity stars: This section presents four nearby hypervelocity star candidates with high probabilities of being unbound from the Milky Way, emphasizing the importance of accurate proper motions and metallicity-dependent distance estimates in determining their escape velocities.

title: Nearby Low-Mass Hypervelocity Stars

catalog_assessment.evidence: Paper presents four identified HVS candidates using SDSS Stripe 82 proper motion data and Palomar spectroscopy, with specific analysis of candidate NHCa.
